days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: First taste of the vibrant city

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Clearing customs and collecting luggage takes a bit of time. Be prepared for the bustling atmosphere.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Icon
timeline.detail: Located near Hung Hom train station, this hotel offers stunning views of Victoria Harbour and modern amenities.

timeline.title: Visit Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade
timeline.detail: Stroll along the waterfront to enjoy panoramic views of the Hong Kong skyline, especially beautiful at sunset.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Mak Mak
timeline.detail: This restaurant offers authentic Thai street food. Don't miss their spicy green curry and mango sticky rice.

timeline.title: Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Watch the spectacular light and sound show from the promenade. The skyline lights up every night at 8 PM.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Relax at Hotel Bar
timeline.detail: Unwind with a cocktail at the hotel’s rooftop bar, enjoying the fantastic views while soaking in the lively atmosphere.

localTips: Purchase an Octopus card for easy transport across public transit, including trains, buses, and ferries.
Consider booking the hotel with a Victoria Harbour view for stunning night scenes.
Check local food blogs for the best street food vendors in Tsim Sha Tsui.

days.title: Exploring Kowloon
days.subtitle: Culinary and cultural immersion

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a Hong Kong-style breakfast of toast, scrambled eggs, and milk tea at this iconic diner.

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Experience one of Hong Kong's most famous temples known for its fortune telling and beautiful architecture.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Enjoy world-renowned dim sum at this Michelin-starred restaurant. Be sure to try their baked bun with BBQ pork.

timeline.title: Explore Mong Kok Markets
timeline.detail: Wander through the vibrant streets of Mong Kok, famous for its street markets, including the Ladies' Market and Goldfish Market.

timeline.title: Dinner at Sister Wah
timeline.detail: Try the best beef brisket noodles in Hong Kong at this local favorite, known for its comforting and hearty bowls.

timeline.title: Visit the Sky100 Observation Deck
timeline.detail: For breathtaking panoramas of the city, head to Sky100 on the 100th floor of the International Commerce Centre.

localTips: Arrive early at dim sum places to avoid long waiting times.
Use a map app to navigate the bustling Mong Kok markets.
Check operating hours as some markets may close early.

days.title: Day Trip to Lantau Island
days.subtitle: Nature and culture combined

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty breakfast buffet at the hotel to prepare for an active day of exploration.

timeline.title: Take Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car
timeline.detail: Breathtaking views of the mountains and sea while ascending to Ngong Ping Village, where the Tian Tan Buddha statue is located.
timeline.mapQuery: Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Tian Tan Buddha
timeline.detail: Explore the iconic giant Buddha and Po Lin Monastery, learn about Buddhism, and enjoy the serene surroundings.

timeline.title: Lunch at The Monastery Vegetarian Restaurant
timeline.detail: Savor a delightful meal of authentic vegetarian dishes at this restaurant within Po Lin Monastery.
timeline.mapQuery: The Monastery Vegetarian Restaurant, Lantau Island

timeline.title: Explore Lantau Island Trails
timeline.detail: Hike one of the trails on Lantau Island, marveling at the unspoiled natural beauty and enjoyable landscapes.

timeline.title: Return to Hong Kong Island
timeline.detail: Take the ferry back to the city, enjoying the sunset over Victoria Harbour while aboard.

localTips: Wear comfortable shoes for hiking and walking around Lantau Island.
Check weather conditions; it can be foggy, affecting visibility.
Try to book cable car tickets in advance to avoid long queues.

days.title: Final Day and Departure
days.subtitle: Last experiences of Hong Kong

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Start your day with a relaxing breakfast before checking out and heading out for last-minute explorations.

timeline.title: Last-minute shopping in Tsim Sha Tsui
timeline.detail: Browse luxury stores and local shops for souvenirs and gifts in the vibrant shopping district.

timeline.title: Lunch at Delicious Kitchen
timeline.detail: Indulge in last-minute local delights at this restaurant popular for its classic wonton noodles and congee.

timeline.title: Check out from Hotel
timeline.detail: Ensure to settle your bill and check out on time to head to the airport.

timeline.title: Transport to Airport
timeline.detail: Use the Airport Express to reach the airport conveniently and quickly, just a quick 25-minute ride.

timeline.title: Depart from Hong Kong
timeline.detail: Board your flight and reflect on the amazing experiences in Hong Kong.

localTips: Allow for extra time at the airport for possible queues during peak hours.
Buy any last-minute souvenirs from the airport shops.
Consider local dessert places for a sweet last bite before leaving.

hotels.name: Hotel Icon
hotels.note: Fantastic Victoria Harbour views, modern amenities
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Estimated total for meals throughout the trip

urgent.label: Book Hotel Icon
urgent.note: Highly recommended for views and comfort.

urgent.label: Purchase Octopus Card
urgent.note: Essential for transport around the city.

_warnings.message: Day 4: "Breakfast at Hotel" also appeared on Day 3 — same venue on consecutive days?
